top_signals.title: Middle East Fuel Exports Rebound as Tankers Slip Through Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Exports of refined fuels from the Persian Gulf have rebounded this month as more tankers managed to slip through the Strait or Hormuz, providing welcome relief to markets starved of supply.

top_signals.title: Trump denies Iran's account of deal terms, decries new drone attack: 'Dishonorable people'
top_signals.summary: "They better get their act together, and FAST!" President Donald Trump said of Iran one day after announcing that a deal would be finalized within days.

top_signals.title: New York is in the Money With Knicks’ Historic Run
top_signals.summary:
The vibes in New York City are very, very good this week with the historic 29-point comeback in Game 4 of the NBA Finals.

Bloomberg's Randall Williams joins Max Chafkin and Stacey Vanek Smith on the Everybody's Business podcast to unpack the NBA frenzy overtaking New York and the economic impact the Knicks' historic run has on the city. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Japan aiming to live up to heightened expectations with deep run at World Cup
top_signals.summary: Japan kicks off its 2026 World Cup on Sunday with a strong, confident squad and the kind of expectations the Samurai Blue have not carried into a tournament for years.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: U.S. natural gas storage capacity increased slightly in 2025
top_signals.summary: Underground working natural gas storage capacity in the Lower 48 states increased slightly in 2025, according to our latest data, with growth concentrated in the South Central and Mountain regions. Underground natural gas storage provides a source of energy when demand increases, balancing U.S. energy needs. We calculate natural gas storage capacity in two ways: demonstrated peak capacity and working gas design capacity. Both increased in 2025.
